query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve (WCSSTUS1):

**Current level (June 2026):**
- Last EIA-confirmed weekly value: **331.2 million barrels** (week ending June 19, 2026)
- Week ending June 26, 2026: **~325.7 million barrels** (per Trading Economics/WPSR) — reportedly the **lowest since May 1983**

**Weekly trajectory (past 3–6 months):** Two clear phases:
1. **Fills/plateau (Dec 2025–Feb 2026):** Rose gradually from ~411.9M to a peak of ~415.4M bbl, then held flat.
2. **Sharp release (late March–June 2026):** Draws began small (~1–4M/wk in April), then accelerated to **8–10 million barrels per week** through May and June. Total drawdown ≈89–90 million barrels from the peak.

**Context:** The release is part of a US policy initiative to release **172 million barrels** to offset global supply shortages following the Iran war and lower fuel prices, amplified by strong exports and high refinery utilization (~96%). EIA confirmed a 7.1M bbl release in the week ending April 24 — the largest single week since October 2022.

**Caveat:** The June 26 figure comes from Trading Economics reporting the EIA WPSR; the EIA historical WCSSTUS1 table directly confirmed values only through June 19. (Note: FRED does not carry this series under the WCSSTUS1 ID — EIA's dnav table is the authoritative source.)

sources.title: DOE has released 17.5 million barrels from the SPR since March
sources.snippet: DOE released 17.5 million barrels between weeks ending March 20 and April 24; 7.1 million bbl in week ending April 24, most since October 7, 2022; SPR at 397.9 million bbl.

sources.title: US Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve Stocks
sources.snippet: 331.19M bbl for week of Jun 19 2026, down from 340.25M prior week and 402.29M one year ago (-17.67% YoY).

sources.title: U.S. commercial crude oil inventories have decreased in June
sources.snippet: Week ending June 19, 2026: refineries at 96.1% utilization; context on crude runs and imports.

sources.title: US Crude Oil Inventories Keep Falling As Hormuz Flows Slow To Start
sources.snippet: Commercial crude ex-SPR shed 59.4 million barrels over eleven weeks; API estimated a 6.072 million barrel crude draw week ending June 26.

sources.snippet: WPSR released June 3, 2026 for week ending May 29 showed a massive combined commercial crude and SPR draw.
